children.name: Hydrobates
children.description: Storm petrels — tiny, bat-like seabirds that patter across the ocean surface with their feet, picking plankton from the water. They are the smallest seabirds in the world, some species weighing as little as 20 grams. Their name comes from their habit of appearing in the lee of ships during storms. They nest in burrows or rock crevices on islands, returning to land only at night.

children.name: Hydrobates pelagicus
children.description: The smallest European seabird, weighing just 25 grams. Sooty-black with a white rump and a square tail. Its erratic, bat-like flight low over the water is distinctive. Breeds in burrows and rock crevices on islands around the British Isles, Iceland, and the Mediterranean. Regularly seen in Swedish waters, especially in autumn.

children.name: Hydrobates leucorhous
children.description: Larger than the European Storm Petrel, with a forked tail and a more bounding flight. Breeds in huge colonies on islands in the North Atlantic and North Pacific. The colony on Baccalieu Island, Newfoundland, is the world's largest seabird colony with over 3 million pairs. Frequently blown inland by autumn storms.

children.name: Hydrobates monorhis
children.description: A small, all-dark storm petrel of the northwest Pacific, breeding on islands off Japan, Korea, and China. Very similar to Leach's but lacks the white rump. An increasing vagrant to European waters, with records in the North Sea and British Isles.

children.name: Hydrobates castro
children.description: A medium-sized storm petrel with a white rump band and a pale bar on the upperwing. Breeds on islands across the Atlantic and Pacific, including the Azores, Madeira, and the Galápagos. Distinguished from Leach's by its less forked tail and different flight action.

children.name: Hydrobates microsoma
children.description: The smallest storm petrel, from Baja California.

children.name: Hydrobates tethys
children.description: A small storm petrel from Peru and Chile.

children.name: Hydrobates melania
children.description: A black storm petrel from California and Mexico.

children.name: Hydrobates matsudairae
children.description: A large storm petrel from the northwest Pacific.

children.name: Hydrobates tristrami
children.description: A storm petrel from the northwest Pacific.

children.name: Hydrobates homochroa
children.description: A storm petrel from California and Mexico.

children.name: Hydrobates furcatus
children.description: A storm petrel with forked tail from the North Pacific.

children.name: Hydrobates monteiroi
children.description: A storm petrel from the Azores.

children.name: Hydrobates jabejabe
children.description: A storm petrel from the Cape Verde Islands.

children.name: Hydrobates macrodactylus
children.description: A possibly extinct storm petrel from Guadalupe Island, Mexico.
